title: Error response from daemon: make cli opts(): making volume mountpoint for volume /XXX/foo/bar: mkdir /XXX: operation not permitted
body:
### Issue Description

Podman Version: 5.2.5
OS: Mac
Using Podman Desktop for Mac


I'm trying to run containers from a compose file that worked fine under Docker. I'm getting the error: `Error response from daemon: make cli opts(): making volume mountpoint for volume /XXX/foo/bar: mkdir /XXX: operation not permitted`

I have tried to use `userns_mode: "keep-id"` in my compose file, I've also set my env with `export PODMAN_USERNS="keep-id"` and neither works nor does any combination of adding UID+GID options. I cannot pass these options as flags to `podman compose` because on Mac the compose extension seems to install the Docker version:

```
>>>> Executing external compose provider "/usr/local/bin/docker-compose". Please see podman-compose(1) for how to disable this message. <<<<

Docker Compose version v2.29.7
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install Podman Desktop for Mac and enable the Compose extension
2. Create a compose file which defines a volume mount for a local directory
3. Run `podman compose up`


### Describe the results you received

```
 podman compose -f compose-dev.yml up
>>>> Executing external compose provider "/usr/local/bin/docker-compose". Please see podman-compose(1) for how to disable this message. <<<<

[+] Running 0/1
 ⠋ Container testcontainer  Creating                                                                                                                             0.1s
Error response from daemon: make cli opts(): making volume mountpoint for volume /my/local/path: mkdir /my: operation not permitted
Error: executing /usr/local/bin/docker-compose -f -compose-dev.yml up: exit status 1
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expect the container to run.

### podman info output

comments.body: What is `/XXX/`? Is that a path mounted into the machine VM? By default the VM only contains mounts for the home directory and some others.

@Luap99 `/XXX/` is a local path mounted into the container. Here's the relevant part of my compose file. The path is specified as relative but the message shows the full path. I have checked permissions and since I am the only user of this Mac and this is my development path, I have the correct permissions and therefore I assume Podman does too.

```yaml
    volumes:
      - "./docker/saml/config/simplesamlphp/authsources.php:/var/www/simplesamlphp/config/authsources.php"
```

Yes but what is `/XXX`? The full path must be mounted into the VM at init time as said above.  If that is not something in your home directory then it is very likely that it is not mounted in the VM so it cannot be used. If a bind mount directory does not exists then tthe podman docker compat API will try to create it. However as the VM uses Fedora coreos the `/` is immutable so we cannot create any new top level directory and thus the error I assume.

You can check the mounts of the VM by using `podman machine ssh` and then check there if you directory exists or not.

As I said, this is a local path that does exist and worked perfectly fine in Docker.

So you are saying you cannot mount just any path in Podman, it has to be in the home directory?

You need to mount the paths you have to use at podman machine init time https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-machine-init.1.html#volume-v-source-target-options

It is irrelevant whenever the paths exists on the host, as long as you did not mount into the VM the podman service cannot see them.
